bottleneck.evidence: result.json per_shape: compute-bound (AI ~1953-2000), candidate MFU 0.158/0.107/0.107 vs ref 0.102/0.027/0.029; on gfx942 aiter's LDS-occupancy heuristic (_gfx942_tile_fits_lds) predicts the default BLOCK_KV=128/ns=2 tile won't keep 2 workgroups co-resident and drops to BLOCK_KV=64/ns=1; grid is (seq_len,) so the seq_len_kv=65536 KV loop dominates and a 64-wide tile serialises it

approaches.summary: Replicate aiter's non-gluon _fp8_mqa_logits_kernel launch exactly (fnuz recast, clean_logits=False buffer+strides, matrix_instr_nonkdim heuristic, weights.squeeze(-1) to (M,32)) but override ONLY BLOCK_KV=256, num_stages=1
approaches.why: BLOCK_KV changes only KV-loop tiling, not the HEAD_SIZE=128 dot reduction, so calc_diff==0.0 (bit-exact); a wider KV tile de-serialises the 65536-long KV loop that the heuristic's 64-wide tile bottlenecked

approaches.summary: Call the kernel without squeezing the 3D weights (M,32,1)
approaches.why: stride unpack stride_w_s, stride_w_h = weights.stride() raises on a 3D tensor -> silent try/except fallback to reference (official ratio ~1.0, looks like correct_not_faster); always confirm the fast path actually runs (calc_diff==0, not raising) before trusting a ~1.0 official ratio

lesson: When the reference is a Triton kernel whose own autotune/occupancy heuristic picks a suboptimal tile on this arch, the safe lever is to call the reference's OWN kernel with its EXACT preprocessing and override ONLY the tile-shape knob (here BLOCK_KV) -- KV-loop tiling doesn't change the head-dim dot reduction so it stays bit-exact. Watch for silent try/except fallbacks that mask a ~1.0 ratio as 'not faster'.
transfers_to: fp8 MQA/attention-logits Triton kernels on gfx942 with long KV and grid=(seq_len,)
any Triton op whose LDS/occupancy heuristic under-sizes the reduction-loop tile on gfx942
caveats: Only valid on the non-gluon gfx942 path (guarded); different arch/Triton>=3.6 gluon path needs re-derivation
Bit-exactness depends on head-dim dot tiling (matrix_instr_nonkdim) staying at the reference heuristic value
